args.name: filter
args.description: A filter expression that filters resources listed in the response. The expression is in the form of field:value. For example, 'instanceType:CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE'. Fields can be nested as needed as per their JSON representation, such as 'settings.userLabels.auto_start:true'. Multiple filter queries are space-separated. For example. 'state:RUNNABLE instanceType:CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE'. By default, each expression is an AND expression. However, you can include AND and OR expressions explicitly.

args.name: maxResults
args.description: The maximum number of instances to return. The service may return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 500 instances are returned. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 are coerced to 1000.

There can be up to three sets of certs listed: the certificate that is currently in use, a future that has been added but not yet used to sign a certificate, and a certificate that has been rotated out. For instances not using Certificate Authority Service (CAS) server CA, use ListServerCas instead.

types.name: Int

types.name: Backup
types.description: A backup resource.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: Output only. This is always `sql#backup`.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. This status indicates whether the backup satisfies PZI. The status is reserved for future use.

fields.name: location
fields.description: The storage location of the backups. The location can be multi-regional.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the backup. Format: projects/{project}/backups/{backup}.

fields.name: expiryTime
fields.description: Backup expiration time. A UTC timestamp of when this backup expired.

fields.name: instance
fields.description: The name of the source database instance.

fields.name: timeZone
fields.description: Output only. This output contains a backup time zone. If a Cloud SQL for SQL Server instance has a different time zone from the backup's time zone, then the restore to the instance doesn't happen.

fields.name: kmsKeyVersion
fields.description: Output only. This output contains the encryption status for a backup and the version of the KMS key that's used to encrypt the Cloud SQL instance.

fields.name: maxChargeableBytes
fields.description: Output only. The maximum chargeable bytes for the backup.

fields.name: instanceDeletionTime
fields.description: Optional. Output only. Timestamp in UTC of when the instance associated with this backup is deleted.

fields.name: selfLink
fields.description: Output only. The URI of this resource.

fields.name: backupRun
fields.description: Output only. The mapping to backup run resource used for IAM validations.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Output only. This status indicates whether the backup satisfies PZS. The status is reserved for future use.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of this backup.

fields.name: kmsKey
fields.description: Output only. This output contains the encryption configuration for a backup and the resource name of the KMS key for disk encryption.

fields.name: ttlDays
fields.description: Input only. The time-to-live (TTL) interval for this resource (in days). For example: ttlDays:7, means 7 days from the current time. The expiration time can't exceed 365 days from the time that the backup is created.

types.name: mutationInput_sql_Backups_UpdateBackup_input_type
types.description: Output only. The type of this backup. The type can be "AUTOMATED", "ON_DEMAND" or “FINAL”.

types.name: mutationInput_sql_Backups_UpdateBackup_input_state
types.description: Output only. The status of this backup.

types.name: Interval
types.description: Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a Timestamp end (exclusive). The start must be less than or equal to the end. When the start equals the end, the interval is empty (matches no time). When both start and end are unspecified, the interval matches any time.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Optional. Exclusive end of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the end.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Optional. Inclusive start of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same or after the start.

types.name: OperationError

fields.name: kind
fields.description: This is always `sql#operationError`.

fields.name: code

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

types.name: mutationInput_sql_Backups_UpdateBackup_input_backupKind
types.description: Output only. Specifies the kind of backup, PHYSICAL or DEFAULT_SNAPSHOT.

types.name: mutationInput_sql_Backups_UpdateBackup_input_databaseVersion
types.description: Output only. The database version of the instance of at the time this backup was made.

For example, a single resource can have the following tags: ``` "123/environment": "production", "123/costCenter": "marketing", ``` For more information on tag creation and management, see https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/tags/tags-overview.

types.name: Settings
types.description: Database instance settings.

fields.name: denyMaintenancePeriods
fields.description: Deny maintenance periods

fields.description: Optional. Whether the replica is in accelerated mode. This feature is in private preview and requires allowlisting to take effect.

fields.name: storageAutoResize
fields.description: Configuration to increase storage size automatically. The default value is true.

fields.name: timeZone
fields.description: Server timezone, relevant only for Cloud SQL for SQL Server.

fields.name: retainBackupsOnDelete
fields.description: Optional. When this parameter is set to true, Cloud SQL retains backups of the instance even after the instance is deleted. The ON_DEMAND backup will be retained until customer deletes the backup or the project. The AUTOMATED backup will be retained based on the backups retention setting.

fields.name: enableGoogleMlIntegration
fields.description: Optional. When this parameter is set to true, Cloud SQL instances can connect to Vertex AI to pass requests for real-time predictions and insights to the AI. The default value is false. This applies only to Cloud SQL for MySQL and Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL instances.

fields.name: authorizedGaeApplications
fields.description: The App Engine app IDs that can access this instance. (Deprecated) Applied to First Generation instances only.

fields.name: dataDiskSizeGb
fields.description: The size of data disk, in GB. The data disk size minimum is 10GB.

fields.name: dataDiskProvisionedIops
fields.description: Optional. Provisioned number of I/O operations per second for the data disk. This field is only used for hyperdisk-balanced disk types.

fields.name: storageAutoResizeLimit
fields.description: The maximum size to which storage capacity can be automatically increased. The default value is 0, which specifies that there is no limit.

fields.name: collation
fields.description: The name of server Instance collation.

fields.name: crashSafeReplicationEnabled
fields.description: Configuration specific to read replica instances. Indicates whether database flags for crash-safe replication are enabled. This property was only applicable to First Generation instances.

fields.name: tier
fields.description: The tier (or machine type) for this instance, for example `db-custom-1-3840`. WARNING: Changing this restarts the instance.

fields.name: deletionProtectionEnabled
fields.description: Configuration to protect against accidental instance deletion.

fields.name: dataDiskProvisionedThroughput
fields.description: Optional. Provisioned throughput measured in MiB per second for the data disk. This field is only used for hyperdisk-balanced disk types.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: This is always `sql#settings`.

fields.name: userLabels
fields.description: User-provided labels, represented as a dictionary where each label is a single key value pair.

fields.name: enableDataplexIntegration
fields.description: Optional. By default, Cloud SQL instances have schema extraction disabled for Dataplex. When this parameter is set to true, schema extraction for Dataplex on Cloud SQL instances is activated.

fields.name: databaseReplicationEnabled
fields.description: Configuration specific to read replica instances. Indicates whether replication is enabled or not. WARNING: Changing this restarts the instance.

fields.name: settingsVersion
fields.description: The version of instance settings. This is a required field for update method to make sure concurrent updates are handled properly. During update, use the most recent settingsVersion value for this instance and do not try to update this value.

fields.name: databaseFlags
fields.description: The database flags passed to the instance at startup.

fields.name: autoUpgradeEnabled
fields.description: Optional. Cloud SQL for MySQL auto-upgrade configuration. When this parameter is set to true, auto-upgrade is enabled for MySQL 8.0 minor versions. The MySQL version must be 8.0.35 or higher.

fields.name: replicationLagMaxSeconds
fields.description: Optional. Configuration value for recreation of replica after certain replication lag

types.name: mutationInput_sql_Backups_UpdateBackup_input_instanceSettings_settings_availabilityType
types.description: Availability type. Potential values: * `ZONAL`: The instance serves data from only one zone. Outages in that zone affect data accessibility. * `REGIONAL`: The instance can serve data from more than one zone in a region (it is highly available)./ For more information, see [Overview of the High Availability Configuration](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/high-availability).

types.name: DenyMaintenancePeriod
types.description: Deny maintenance Periods. This specifies a date range during when all CSA rollout will be denied.

fields.name: time
fields.description: Time in UTC when the "deny maintenance period" starts on start_date and ends on end_date. The time is in format: HH:mm:SS, i.e., 00:00:00

fields.name: endDate
fields.description: "deny maintenance period" end date. If the year of the end date is empty, the year of the start date also must be empty. In this case, it means the no maintenance interval recurs every year. The date is in format yyyy-mm-dd i.e., 2020-11-01, or mm-dd, i.e., 11-01

fields.name: startDate
fields.description: "deny maintenance period" start date. If the year of the start date is empty, the year of the end date also must be empty. In this case, it means the deny maintenance period recurs every year. The date is in format yyyy-mm-dd i.e., 2020-11-01, or mm-dd, i.e., 11-01

fields.name: enhancedQueryInsightsEnabled
fields.description: Optional. Whether enhanced query insights feature is enabled.

fields.name: queryInsightsEnabled
fields.description: Whether Query Insights feature is enabled.

fields.name: recordApplicationTags

fields.name: queryStringLength
fields.description: Maximum query length stored in bytes. Default value: 1024 bytes. Range: 256-4500 bytes. Query lengths greater than this field value will be truncated to this value. When unset, query length will be the default value. Changing query length will restart the database.

fields.name: recordClientAddress

types.name: mutationInput_sql_Backups_UpdateBackup_input_instanceSettings_settings_ipConfiguration_sslMode
types.description: Specify how SSL/TLS is enforced in database connections. If you must use the `require_ssl` flag for backward compatibility, then only the following value pairs are valid: For PostgreSQL and MySQL: * `ssl_mode=ALLOW_UNENCRYPTED_AND_ENCRYPTED` and `require_ssl=false` * `ssl_mode=ENCRYPTED_ONLY` and `require_ssl=false` * `ssl_mode=TRUSTED_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_REQUIRED` and `require_ssl=true` For SQL Server: * `ssl_mode=ALLOW_UNENCRYPTED_AND_ENCRYPTED` and `require_ssl=false` * `ssl_mode=ENCRYPTED_ONLY` and `require_ssl=true` The value of `ssl_mode` has priority over the value of `require_ssl`. For example, for the pair `ssl_mode=ENCRYPTED_ONLY` and `require_ssl=false`, `ssl_mode=ENCRYPTED_ONLY` means accept only SSL connections, while `require_ssl=false` means accept both non-SSL and SSL connections. In this case, MySQL and PostgreSQL databases respect `ssl_mode` and accepts only SSL connections.

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: Output only. This is always `sql#backup`.

inputFields.description: Output only. This status indicates whether the backup satisfies PZI. The status is reserved for future use.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: The storage location of the backups. The location can be multi-regional.

inputFields.description: Output only. The resource name of the backup. Format: projects/{project}/backups/{backup}.

inputFields.name: expiryTime
inputFields.description: Backup expiration time. A UTC timestamp of when this backup expired.

inputFields.description: The name of the source database instance.

inputFields.name: timeZone
inputFields.description: Output only. This output contains a backup time zone. If a Cloud SQL for SQL Server instance has a different time zone from the backup's time zone, then the restore to the instance doesn't happen.

inputFields.name: kmsKeyVersion
inputFields.description: Output only. This output contains the encryption status for a backup and the version of the KMS key that's used to encrypt the Cloud SQL instance.

inputFields.name: maxChargeableBytes
inputFields.description: Output only. The maximum chargeable bytes for the backup.

inputFields.name: instanceDeletionTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Output only. Timestamp in UTC of when the instance associated with this backup is deleted.

inputFields.name: selfLink
inputFields.description: Output only. The URI of this resource.

inputFields.name: backupRun
inputFields.description: Output only. The mapping to backup run resource used for IAM validations.

inputFields.name: satisfiesPzs
inputFields.description: Output only. This status indicates whether the backup satisfies PZS. The status is reserved for future use.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of this backup.

inputFields.name: kmsKey
inputFields.description: Output only. This output contains the encryption configuration for a backup and the resource name of the KMS key for disk encryption.

inputFields.name: ttlDays
inputFields.description: Input only. The time-to-live (TTL) interval for this resource (in days). For example: ttlDays:7, means 7 days from the current time. The expiration time can't exceed 365 days from the time that the backup is created.

types.name: Interval_Input
types.description: Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a Timestamp end (exclusive). The start must be less than or equal to the end. When the start equals the end, the interval is empty (matches no time). When both start and end are unspecified, the interval matches any time.

inputFields.name: endTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Exclusive end of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the end.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Inclusive start of the interval. If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same or after the start.

[RFC 3339](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339) format (for example, `2023-10-01T16:19:00.094`) in UTC. When omitted, all available logs from the beginning of retention period will be included. Only applied to Cloud SQL for SQL Server.

inputFields.name: exportLogEndTime
inputFields.description: Optional. The end timestamp when transaction log will be included in the export operation. [RFC 3339](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339) format (for example, `2023-10-01T16:19:00.094`) in UTC. When omitted, all available logs until current time will be included. Only applied to Cloud SQL for SQL Server.

types.name: Status
types.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

fields.name: message

fields.name: details

fields.name: code

types.name: String

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
